steps.title: Create two points from the slope
steps.content: The graph passes through $(0,0)$. A slope of $-54$ means that when $x$ increases from $0$ to $1$, $y$ decreases to $-54$. Use the points $(0,0)$ and $(1,-54)$.

steps.title: Fit the line
steps.content: Run $y_1 \sim mx_1+b$ in Desmos. The regression gives $m=-54$ and $b=0$, so the equation is $y=-54x$.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The fitted equation is $f(x)=-54x$, which matches choice A. The other choices have slopes $-36$, $-18$, and $1$, so they cannot match the given slope. Choice A is correct.
